memes.label: "The books that the world calls immoral are books that show the world its own shame"
memes.description: "Lord Henry Wotton rejects the idea that books can corrupt, arguing that so-called immoral books merely reflect society's own vices."

memes.label: The Bristol Hotel
memes.description: A discreet London hotel where Dorian Gray and Lord Henry Wotton hold private dinners, serving as a venue for their most intimate and influential conversations.

memes.label: The Bristol Hotel
memes.description: A discreet London hotel where Lord Henry Wotton, Dorian Gray, and Basil Hallward dine privately — the setting for key conversations about Sibyl Vane's engagement and Dorian's first declaration of love, embodying the novel's culture of privileged private dining.

memes.label: The Anglican Church
memes.description: The Church of England, the established Christian church in Victorian Britain, which Dorian Gray turns to in a brief and ultimately hollow attempt at moral reformation.

memes.label: The Club
memes.description: The unnamed gentlemen's club in London's West End that serves as Lord Henry Wotton's primary domain — the masculine, aristocratic space where reputation is made and unmade, and the counterpoint to the novel's domestic and theatrical settings.

memes.label: "The costume of the nineteenth century is detestable"
memes.description: "Lord Henry Wotton dismisses modern dress as 'sombre' and 'depressing,' using aesthetic disgust to set up his aphorism that sin is the only remaining source of colour in modern life."

memes.label: The Curzon Street House
memes.description: Lord Henry Wotton's Mayfair residence on Curzon Street — the intellectual epicentre from which his philosophy of aesthetic individualism radiates, where Dorian Gray first comes under his influence, and the drawing room where the novel's most dangerous ideas are refined into wit.

memes.label: "The dead linger sometimes"
memes.description: "Dorian Gray, desperate to dispose of Basil Hallward's body, tells Alan Campbell that the dead do not simply vanish — a line that distills the novel's central truth: the past cannot be escaped."

relations.label: It had been like conscience to him

memes.label: The East End
memes.description: The impoverished East End district of Victorian London — the object of Lady Agatha's philanthropy, Dorian Gray's early charitable impulses, and Lord Henry Wotton's amused indifference, symbolizing the social conscience that the novel's aristocratic world both acknowledges and dismisses.

memes.label: The Grosvenor Gallery
memes.description: The avant-garde London art gallery where Basil Hallward intends to exhibit Dorian Gray's portrait — a real institution of the Aesthetic Movement that Wilde uses to situate the novel in contemporary artistic culture.

memes.label: "The husbands of very beautiful women belong to the criminal classes"
memes.description: "Lord Henry's dinner-party epigram on marriage and beauty — a characteristically Wildean inversion of conventional morality delivered over wine at Lady Narborough's table."

relations.label: Nowadays people know the price of everything and the value of nothing.
The only way to get rid of a temptation is to yield to it.
Women love us for our defects.

memes.label: The Living Death of His Own Soul
memes.description: The narrator's diagnosis of what truly troubles Dorian Gray — not the deaths he has caused, but the ongoing death of his own soul, a condition far worse than physical death.
aliases: it was the living death of his own soul that troubled him

memes.label: The Man Who Could Call a Spade a Spade
memes.description: "The man who could call a spade a spade should be compelled to use one. It is the only thing he is fit for."

memes.label: "The One Charm of Marriage"
memes.description: Lord Henry Wotton's epigram on marriage — "the one charm of marriage is that it makes a life of deception absolutely necessary for both parties" — a quintessential expression of his cynical wit spoken to Basil Hallward in Chapter 1.

memes.label: The One Charm of the Past
memes.description: Lord Henry reflects on the peculiar relationship between women and the past — "The one charm of the past is that it is the past. But women never know when the curtain has fallen."

memes.label: The Only Horrible Thing Is Ennui
memes.description: "Lord Henry declares boredom the only unforgivable sin: 'The only horrible thing in the world is ennui, Dorian. That is the one sin for which there is no forgiveness.'"
aliases: only horrible thing in the world is ennui

memes.label: The Only Way to Get Rid of a Temptation
memes.description: Lord Henry's most famous paradox — that resistance to temptation is itself a sickness, and that yielding is purification — the central precept of the new Hedonism he preaches to Dorian Gray in Chapter 2.
aliases: the only way to get rid of a temptation is to yield to it
resist it and your soul grows sick with longing

memes.label: The Opium Den
memes.description: The squalid East End establishment where Dorian Gray seeks oblivion and where James Vane nearly achieves his revenge — the novel's descent into London's underworld.

memes.label: The Orleans Club
memes.description: A fashionable London gentlemen's club frequented by Lord Henry Wotton, mentioned in connection with his social engagements and the fabric of upper-class male society.

memes.label: The Past
memes.description: A spectral force in the novel — the past is never truly gone but continually returns to haunt the present, whether through memory, the portrait, or the reappearance of James Vane. (006-058)

memes.label: The Past Could Always Be Annihilated
memes.description: "Narrative aphorism on Dorian's attempt to escape his past: the past can be erased, but the future is inescapable."

memes.label: The Portrait
memes.description: The enchanted painting of Dorian Gray by Basil Hallward — the novel's central symbolic object that bears the marks of age and sin while its subject remains outwardly unchanged.

memes.label: The Portrait's First Transformation
memes.description: After Dorian Gray cruelly rejects Sibyl Vane, Basil Hallward's portrait of him shows its first supernatural change — a touch of cruelty in the mouth — confirming that Dorian's wish has come true.

memes.label: The Prayer of Your Pride Has Been Answered
memes.description: "The prayer of your pride has been answered. The prayer of your repentance will be answered also. I worshipped you too much. I am punished for it. You worshipped yourself too much. We are both punished."

memes.label: Nowadays People Know the Price of Everything
memes.description: "Lord Henry's famous epigram on modern materialism: people know the cost of things but not their worth."

memes.label: The London Season
memes.description: The annual London social calendar that structured the lives of the Victorian upper classes, governing when society appeared in town, held balls and dinners, and retreated to country estates — the rhythm that frames much of the novel's social world.

memes.label: The Secret of Life Is to Reproduce That Experience
memes.description: Lord Henry's aesthetic philosophy of love — that life offers only one great experience, and the secret of living is to repeat it as often as possible.
aliases: we can have in life but one great experience

memes.label: The Secret of Remaining Young
memes.description: Lord Henry's formula for eternal youth as emotional discipline — "The secret of remaining young is never to have an emotion that is unbecoming."
aliases: never to have an emotion that is unbecoming

memes.label: "He used to wonder at the shallow psychology of those who conceive the ego as a thing simple"
memes.description: "The narrator articulates Dorian Gray's conviction that the self is not a simple, permanent unity but a multiple, ever-changing entity — a philosophy that licenses his double life."

relations.label: Form is absolutely essential to it.
Each of us has Heaven and Hell in him.

memes.label: The Terror of Society, the Basis of Morals
memes.description: "Lord Henry's diagnosis of the forces that govern human behaviour — not virtue or conviction, but fear: fear of society and fear of God — setting the stage for his argument that self-realization requires courage."
aliases: the two things that govern us

memes.label: The Theatre
memes.description: The shabby Holborn playhouse where Sibyl Vane performs Shakespeare — the setting for Dorian's courtship, disillusionment, and first act of cruelty, symbolizing the permeable boundary between art and life.

memes.label: "The Tragedy of Old Age Is Not That One Is Old, but That One Is Young"
memes.description: "Lord Henry's reversal of conventional wisdom on aging — \"The tragedy of old age is not that one is old, but that one is young\" — a paradox that reveals the novel's deepest anxiety about time, beauty, and the persistence of desire."
aliases: not that one is old but that one is young

memes.label: The tragedy of old age
memes.description: "The tragedy of old age is not that one is old, but that one is young."

memes.label: The Value of an Idea Has Nothing Whatsoever to Do with the Sincerity of the Man Who Expresses It
memes.description: "Lord Henry's defense of intellectual play: an idea's worth is independent of its speaker's belief in it."

memes.label: The Vane Family
memes.description: The modest theatrical family whose daughter Sibyl's romance with Dorian Gray and subsequent suicide set in motion the novel's central arc of guilt, retribution, and moral consequence.

memes.label: The world goes to the altar of its own accord
memes.description: "The world goes to the altar of its own accord."

relations.label: You would sacrifice anybody, Harry, for the sake of an epigram

memes.label: Made of Ivory and Gold
memes.description: The ultimate tribute to Dorian Gray's beauty, recalled at the moment of his despair — "The world is changed because you are made of ivory and gold. The curves of your lips rewrite history."
aliases: curves of your lips rewrite history

relations.label: His beauty had been to him but a mask, his youth but a mockery

memes.label: The Yellow Book
memes.description: The poisonous French novel sent by Lord Henry to Dorian Gray — widely identified as Joris-Karl Huysmans's À Rebours (1884) — which becomes a profound influence on Dorian's life and philosophy. (010-042)

memes.label: Two Kinds of Fascinating People
memes.description: Lord Henry's paradoxical taxonomy of human fascination — "There are only two kinds of people who are really fascinating—people who know absolutely everything, and people who know absolutely nothing."
aliases: people who know everything and nothing

memes.label: "There Goes the Devil's Bargain!"
memes.description: "The opium woman's hoarse cry as Dorian Gray leaves the den — a street-level revelation that confirms the supernatural truth of his bargain: he has sold himself to the devil for a pretty face."

memes.label: There Is a Luxury in Self-Reproach
memes.description: "There is a luxury in self-reproach. When we blame ourselves, we feel that no one else has a right to blame us. It is the confession, not the priest, that gives us absolution."

memes.label: There Is Always Something Infinitely Mean About Other People's Tragedies
memes.description: Lord Henry's cold aphorism on the fundamental indifference we feel toward the suffering of others, delivered in response to Dorian's rebuke about neglecting Sibyl Vane's history in Chapter 4.
aliases: there is always something infinitely mean about other people's tragedies

memes.label: Something Ridiculous
memes.description: The narrator's devastating moral observation — "There is always something ridiculous about the emotions of people whom one has ceased to love."

memes.label: There Is No One with Whom I Would Not Change Places
memes.description: Dorian Gray's terrified confession to Lord Henry — that he would trade places with anyone, even a dead peasant, because the anticipation of death is worse than death itself.
aliases: i have no terror of death
the coming of death terrifies me

memes.label: There Is No Such Thing as a Good Influence
memes.description: Lord Henry's foundational paradox — that all influence is immoral because it imposes one soul upon another — spoken to Dorian Gray during their first meeting in Basil Hallward's studio.
aliases: no such thing as a good influence

memes.label: There Is Only One Thing Worse Than Being Talked About
memes.description: "Lord Henry's aphorism on the primacy of notoriety: the only thing worse than scandal is obscurity."

memes.label: There Is Something Fatal About a Portrait
memes.description: Dorian Gray's ominous refusal to sit for Basil Hallward again — the first sign that the portrait has become a source of unease rather than pride — in Chapter 9.
aliases: there is something fatal about a portrait

memes.label: There Was Purification in Punishment
memes.description: "Dorian Gray's final reflection before destroying the portrait: 'There was purification in punishment. Not Forgive us our sins but Smite us for our iniquities should be the prayer of man to a most just God.'"

memes.label: There Were Sins Whose Fascination
memes.description: "Narrator: 'There were sins whose fascination was more in the memory than in the doing of them' — a psychological insight that distinguishes Dorian's murder from the aesthetic sins of his past."

memes.label: "They would mar its beauty"
memes.description: "Dorian Gray foresees that his sins will disfigure the portrait — marring its beauty and eating away its grace, a vision of sin as aesthetic destruction."

relations.label: What the worm was to the corpse
Sin is a thing that writes itself

memes.label: "The things one feels absolutely certain about are never true"
memes.description: "Lord Henry Wotton declares that absolute certainty is the mark of illusion, articulating the Decadent skepticism that defines his worldview."

relations.label: Life is not governed by will or intention
I can sympathize with everything except suffering.
The terror of society, which is the basis of morals...

memes.label: This world and I are on excellent terms
memes.description: "\"It can only be the next world. This world and I are on excellent terms.\""

memes.label: To Be Good Is to Be in Harmony with One's Self
memes.description: Lord Henry redefines goodness as internal consistency rather than external conformity — "To be good is to be in harmony with one's self. Discord is to be forced to be in harmony with others."

memes.label: "To be in love is to surpass one's self"
memes.description: "Sibyl Vane declares the transformative power of love as she prepares to perform Juliet before Dorian Gray."

relations.label: love flies in through the window

memes.label: To Be Popular One Must Be a Mediocrity
memes.description: "To be popular one must be a mediocrity."

memes.label: To Become the Spectator of One's Own Life
memes.description: "Dorian Gray articulates Lord Henry's philosophy of detachment: 'To become the spectator of one's own life is to escape the suffering of life.'"

memes.label: To Cure the Soul by Means of the Senses
memes.description: "Dorian Gray, in a hansom cab on his way to an opium den, repeats Lord Henry's words — 'To cure the soul by means of the senses, and the senses by means of the soul!' — the day after murdering Basil Hallward."
aliases: cure the soul by the senses

memes.label: To Define Is to Limit
memes.description: Lord Henry Wotton's aphorism "To define is to limit" — a late-novel statement that encapsulates his philosophy of refusal to be pinned down by categories or moral labels.

memes.label: "To reveal art and conceal the artist is art's aim"
memes.description: "The opening declaration of Wilde's Preface: art should stand on its own, independent of the artist's biography, intentions, or moral character."

memes.label: "To see him is to worship him; to know him is to trust him"
memes.description: "Sibyl Vane's declaration of absolute devotion to Dorian Gray, framing love as both aesthetic and religious experience."
aliases: to know him is to trust him

relations.label: To be in love is to surpass one's self
I shudder at the thought of being free

memes.label: "To see my soul!"
memes.description: "Dorian Gray's terrified exclamation when Basil Hallward says he would need to see Dorian's soul — the turning point that leads to the revelation of the portrait."

relations.label: Sin is a thing that writes itself

memes.label: "To Spiritualize One's Age"
memes.description: Basil Hallward's tribute to Sibyl Vane's power — "To spiritualize one's age — that is something worth doing" — an expression of his idealist faith that art can transform life, spoken in Chapter 7.

memes.label: Transformation
memes.description: The progressive moral and psychological change that Dorian Gray undergoes — from innocent youth to corrupt hedonist — driven by influence, enabled by the portrait, and culminating in self-destruction.

memes.label: Only Two Eras of Any Importance in the World's History
memes.description: "Basil Hallward's declaration that history is defined by two things: the appearance of a new medium for art, and the appearance of a new personality for art."

memes.label: Ugliness
memes.description: The aesthetic and moral antithesis of beauty in Wilde's novel — the visible sign of inner corruption made manifest on the portrait's canvas. (010-040)

memes.label: Ugliness Is One of the Seven Deadly Sins
memes.description: The Duchess of Monmouth's witty challenge to Lord Henry — "Ugliness is one of the seven deadly sins, then?" — drawing out the aesthetic moral code that equates beauty with virtue.
aliases: ugliness is one of the seven deadly sins then

memes.label: Ugliness Is One of the Seven Deadly Virtues
memes.description: "Lord Henry inverts the Christian schema of the seven deadly sins, declaring ugliness a virtue that — with beer and the Bible — has made England."
aliases: beer the bible and the seven deadly virtues

memes.label: "Ugliness was the one reality"
memes.description: "In the opium den, Dorian Gray undergoes a radical inversion of values: ugliness, once hateful to him, becomes dear because it is real — a striking reversal of the aestheticist creed he once lived by."

memes.label: Vanity
memes.description: Excessive pride in one's appearance — the moral weakness that makes Dorian Gray vulnerable to Lord Henry's philosophy and drives his desperate attempt to preserve his beauty at any cost.

memes.label: Victor
memes.description: Dorian Gray's French valet, whose silent domestic service in Chapters 8, 10, and 13 provides an unblinking witness to his master's increasingly secretive life.

memes.label: Victoria Wotton
memes.description: Lord Henry Wotton's wife, mentioned only in passing—a woman described as having "a perfect mania for going to church" whose separation from her husband illustrates the Wottons' marriage of convenience.

memes.label: The Visible Emblem of Conscience
memes.description: Dorian Gray reflects that the portrait would serve as "the visible emblem of conscience" — a pivotal line marking his awareness of the painting's moral function.

memes.label: Was There Anything So Real as Words?
memes.description: Dorian Gray's internal reflection on the terrifying and transformative power of language — the moment he realizes that words can reshape reality, and that Lord Henry's speech has permanently altered him.

memes.label: We Are Both Punished
memes.description: Basil Hallward's prayer of recognition before the corrupted portrait — "I worshipped you too much. I am punished for it. You worshipped yourself too much. We are both punished."

memes.label: We Are in the Native Land of the Hypocrite
memes.description: Lord Henry's sardonic observation that England is the natural home of hypocrisy — a pointed critique of Victorian society delivered in the midst of mounting scandal around Dorian Gray.
aliases: the native land of the hypocrite

memes.label: West End
memes.description: The fashionable district of London, home to theatres and exclusive clubs, representing the world of wealth and status in Victorian society (004-087).

memes.label: "What the worm was to the corpse"
memes.description: "Dorian Gray reflects that his sins will corrupt the portrait as a worm corrupts a corpse — a metaphor for the inexorable decay of the painted soul."

relations.label: Sin is a thing that writes itself

memes.label: What Was Youth at Best
memes.description: The narrator's devastating final assessment of youth — no longer a treasure but a green, unripe, shallow time — as Dorian Gray crushes the mirror that once flattered him.
aliases: a time of shallow moods and sickly thoughts

memes.label: When a woman marries again
memes.description: "\"When a woman marries again, it is because she detested her first husband. When a man marries again, it is because he adored his first wife.\""

memes.label: When One Is In Love One Always Begins By Deceiving Ones Self
memes.description: Lord Henry's cynical definition of romance as self-deception followed by the deception of others, delivered during a conversation with Dorian about Sibyl Vane in Chapter 4.
aliases: when one is in love one always begins by deceiving one's self

memes.label: When Poverty Creeps in at the Door
memes.description: Sibyl Vane's ecstatic revision of a proverb — "When poverty creeps in at the door, love flies in through the window" — as she declares her love for Dorian Gray to her brother James.
aliases: love flies in through the window

memes.label: Whenever a Man Does a Thoroughly Stupid Thing
memes.description: "Lord Henry's cynical aphorism on motivation: whenever a man does something thoroughly stupid, he does it from the noblest motives."

memes.label: Whitechapel
memes.description: A poor East End district of Victorian London — the site of Lady Agatha's charitable club and, later, of the rumored criminal dens where Dorian Gray's reputation darkens, tracing the novel's arc from earnest philanthropy to secret degradation.

memes.label: "Without her you would have been incomplete"
memes.description: "Basil Hallward tells Dorian that Sibyl Vane was made for him — without her, he would have been incomplete — expressing a romantic faith in destined love that Lord Henry immediately undercuts."

memes.label: Without Your Art You Are Nothing
memes.description: Dorian Gray's cruelest verdict on Sibyl Vane — "Without your art, you are nothing. I would have made you famous, splendid, magnificent. What are you now? A third-rate actress with a pretty face."

memes.label: Women Are a Decorative Sex
memes.description: Lord Henry Wotton's dismissal of feminine intellect — "Women are a decorative sex" — in response to Dorian's claim that Sibyl Vane is a genius.

memes.label: "Women defend themselves by attacking, just as they attack by sudden and strange surrenders"
memes.description: "The narrator's aphoristic observation of Mrs Vane's behaviour encapsulates Wilde's characteristic epigrammatic style — a chiasmus that turns a psychological insight into a symmetrical paradox."

memes.label: "Women love us for our defects"
memes.description: "Lord Henry Wotton declares that women are drawn to men's flaws, suggesting that imperfection — not virtue — is the true engine of romantic attachment."

memes.label: Women Love with Their Ears
memes.description: "We women, as some one says, love with our ears, just as you men love with your eyes, if you ever love at all."

memes.label: Worse Than Being Talked About
memes.description: "Lord Henry's aphorism on reputation and society: there is only one thing worse than being talked about, and that is not being talked about."
aliases: only thing worse than being talked about

memes.label: Worship
memes.description: The intense, quasi-religious adoration that Basil Hallward feels for Dorian Gray — an artistic and personal devotion that blurs the line between aesthetic appreciation and dangerous idealization.

memes.label: "You are infamous, absolutely infamous!"
memes.description: "Alan Campbell, forced to dispose of Basil Hallward's body, looks at Dorian Gray's beautiful, tearful face and delivers the novel's most direct moral condemnation of its protagonist."

relations.label: You have gone from corruption to corruption, and now you have culminated in crime
His beauty had been to him but a mask, his youth but a mockery

memes.label: You Are Quite Perfect
memes.description: Lord Henry Wotton's greeting to Dorian Gray in Chapter 19 — "You are quite perfect. Pray, don't change" — devastatingly ironic given the corruption Dorian has just confessed.
aliases: you are quite perfect pray don't change

memes.label: "You cut life to pieces with your epigrams"
memes.description: "Dorian Gray accuses Lord Henry Wotton of reducing life to fragments with his witty aphorisms, an ironic reproach given how thoroughly Dorian will come to embody those very epigrams."

memes.label: You Have Explained Me to Myself
memes.description: Dorian Gray's confession to Lord Henry after Sibyl Vane's suicide — that Harry's words have named feelings Dorian could not articulate, a moment of grateful self-recognition that marks the beginning of his corruption.
aliases: i felt all that you have said but was afraid of it

memes.label: You Have Killed My Love
memes.description: Dorian Gray cruelly rejects Sibyl Vane after her uninspired performance — "You have killed my love. You used to stir my imagination. Now you don't even stir my curiosity."

memes.label: You Used to Stir My Imagination
memes.description: Dorian Gray's cruel renunciation of Sibyl Vane — he loved her for her art, and when she could no longer act, she became nothing to him.
aliases: now you dont even stir my curiosity

memes.label: You Will Always Be Fond of Me
memes.description: 'Lord Henry tells Dorian: "You will always be fond of me. I represent to you all the sins you have never had the courage to commit."'
aliases: sins you never had the courage to commit

memes.label: You Will Always Be in Love with Love
memes.description: "Lord Henry to Dorian: You will always be loved, and you will always be in love with love. A grande passion is the privilege of people who have nothing to do."

memes.label: "You would sacrifice anybody, Harry, for the sake of an epigram"
memes.description: "Dorian Gray indicts Lord Henry's epigrammatic habit as a form of moral indifference — wit at the expense of human feeling."

relations.label: The world goes to the altar of its own accord

memes.label: "You Wrecked the Life of Sibyl Vane"
memes.description: James Vane's direct accusation of Dorian Gray — the moment when the revenge plot of Chapter 16 crystallizes into a single, devastating sentence that names the crime and the criminal.

memes.label: Young People Imagine Money Is Everything
memes.description: Lord Fermor's cynical observation on the illusions of youth — that the young mistake money for everything, while the old know better through experience — spoken in the opening of Lord Henry's visit to his uncle.
aliases: young people nowadays imagine that money is everything

memes.label: Youth
memes.description: The transient period of life that Dorian Gray desperately seeks to preserve — the source of his beauty, the object of his terror, and the catalyst for his Faustian bargain.

memes.label: Youth Is the One Thing Worth Having
memes.description: Lord Henry's opening declaration in his celebrated speech on youth — the assertion that launches Dorian Gray's fatal preoccupation with his own beauty and mortality.

memes.label: Youth Smiles Without Reason
memes.description: The narrator's reflection on the amorality of youth — that it smiles "without any reason" — moments after Dorian Gray wakes from untroubled sleep on the morning after murdering Basil Hallward.
